author: agenticbranduniverse.com/registry
description: An illustrated, narrated picture book. The reference hard case: identity must hold across every spread.

surface.note: A picture-book SPREAD is landscape (3:2). On the full-spread reader one landscape illustration spans both pages, so a portrait spread gets cropped into a landscape frame and loses the tops of heads. The COVER and the closing PLATE are single portrait pages (2:3); their per-slot geometry overrides this. A book delivered art-and-text (portrait art beside text) overrides the spread geometry per composition. Earned 2026-07-23: The Outcome Economy shipped with every spread sized portrait against a full-spread manifest.

slots.note: The cover is a single PORTRAIT page (2:3) and carries the title, the author, and any series line. Every book cover ever printed does. A blanket no-text rule turns the ONE slot that must have type into a defect.

A spread is LANDSCAPE (3:2). On the full-spread reader it spans both pages. Portrait art here is cropped and loses heads. Text is PERMITTED: the reader overlays the beat caption itself, but a blanket 'no text, no letters, no numbers' rule distorts the scene (blank laptop screens, textless signs, wordless book pages) and is worse than the incidental text it prevents. Earned 2026-07-23. The judged invariant 'the spread's beat text is legible and unmangled' remains the backstop against garbled type.
The closing plate is a single PORTRAIT page (2:3), like the cover. An explanatory plate woven into the narrative. Characterless by contract.

generators.pinReason: the locked character golden carries identity
A locked character golden carries the identity, so any competent provider works.

quirks.seen: When a person is depicted drawing, holding, or displaying a picture, the depicted picture renders upside down relative to the viewer.
quirks.counter: Any picture, page, book, drawing, or artwork shown INSIDE the scene must be RIGHT SIDE UP and correctly oriented to the viewer. Never inverted, never rotated, never upside down.

perSlot.id: flat: no perspective, shading, gradients, or coloured linework
the spread's beat text is legible and unmangled

crossSlot.id: every declared invariant of each bound character holds in every slot
crossSlot.scope: all slots binding the same character entity
crossSlot.against: the entity's locked master golden, never slot-to-slot
crossSlot.method: pass the locked master AND its written locked description into every slot; judge each slot in fresh context against the master, item by item over the entity's invariant list, never against the plan
crossSlot.note: Itemized because a gestalt 'same character?' passes while a specific invariant fails. Against the golden because slots drift together and read as consistent.

notes: Runtime requirement: this is the projection whose run is long enough that nobody watches it. Park a defective spread, finish the rest, report.
